key_insight: Localized carrier spins in metal-halide perovskites relax mainly through hyperfine coupling, with holes typically experiencing stronger relaxation than electrons due to the material’s inverted band structure.
engineering_relevance: Understanding and tuning hyperfine-driven spin lifetimes can guide perovskite composition choices for more stable spintronic and optoelectronic devices.
method: Adapted a muon-spin relaxation model to carrier spin dynamics.
Solved the longitudinal spin relaxation exactly across arbitrary hyperfine correlation times.
Applied the model to time-resolved Faraday rotation to extract localization volume and correlation time.
evidence_signal: The model was tested against time-resolved Faraday rotation data from two perovskite samples and outperformed a single-exponential approximation in the intermediate correlation regime.
watch_item: The framework targets localized carriers, so its predictions may be less accurate for delocalized transport regimes or samples with strong disorder variation.
paper_title: Hyperfine driven spin relaxation of charge carriers in metal-halide perovskites
